item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image originates from here, which maintains all content under an All Rights Reserved license. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: author approved of edits via pm, timer removed

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

From what I can tell, this skip involves both Prometheus Labs and Dr. Wondertainment. I propose linking both of their hubs to here.

Editing this:

[DATA LOST]rom[DATA LOST] La[DATA LOST]. We bring the Light to you.

To this:

[DATA LOST]rom[DATA LOST] La[DATA LOST]. We bring the Light to you.

Editing this:

Hello, and thank you for purchasing "Dr. Wondertainment's Slinktastic Dancerific Rainbow Friend!™"

To this:

Hello, and thank you for purchasing "Dr. Wondertainment's Slinktastic Dancerific Rainbow Friend!™"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addenda killed it for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[DATA LOST]scaped from PL security.

PL - "Prometheus Labs, Inc."?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems like hibernation could be used to make even safer containment procedures. Crank up the heat and just keep it asleep except as needed to feed it. No more risk of it even accidentally injuring anyone.
At the very least, exploiting hibernation should be mentioned as a contingency re-containment plan.

You've got a good characterization of Dr. W there, and I hate it. W works best as a slapstick, wonka-esque, enigma; when we aren't even sure if it's a him, a fictional mascot, or an eldritch abomination from beyond the stars. Humanizing him takes away a large portion of the wonder, which makes the GoI less entertaining.

But I have no issues with the item itself, so it cancels out to a neutral-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually I saw a video of this dude a couple of weeks ago: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BKhNOUfmnyU

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1544 will consume any type of meat, and is to be fed daily.

How much meat? Might be useful.

However, SCP-1544 will hibernate at temperatures exceeding 85° C to preserve energy and prevent its interior from overheating.

Is this really necessary for inclusion? As far as I can tell, it adds nothing to the story of the thing.

otherwise, SCP-1544 will display depression, and will lay or sit down on the ground away from personnel

'lay' should be 'lie'.

Those minor problems aside, I liked this one a lot. Tragic and funny at the same time - I'm a particular fan of how he gets depressed when no music is played. Some nice fridge brilliance here - how do you know he's not always depressed, and it's just that when music plays, he can't help but dance?

I like the middle note as well, though I'm not entirely certain I got it totally. Some company was trying to engineer something sneaky and stealthy, and accidentally made this instead? DW took it and put it in an exoskeleton made of slinkies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really like the last note from DW, just sort of hits me right in the heart. Also like the idea as a whole, and all I keep thinking of a sentient inflatable tube man

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I feel bad for it! IT JUST WANTS TO DANCE!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few points.

First, I've said this before: the "TM" should only be on the name of the product, not after "Dr Wondertainment". Otherwise it lessens the ambiguity — plus, you're portraying Dr Wondertainment here as being a person.

Second, oh dear, a URL. Last time there was an SCP that involved a URL, I warned the author that we'd be at risk of having it be domain-squatted, and within a few weeks that's exactly what happened. Redact the TLD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not particularly interesting, and I feel like it's got some tonal issues.

But hey, Mummenschanz!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Went through and fixed those errors. Thanks for pointing them out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor grammatical issues:

+ Really minor
- Really minor

SCP-1544's exterior is a mobile, 1 meter tall multicolored skin covering. It is 1 meter in height, and is controlled by a small, peach colored mass located in the center.

You're repeating the height in two sentences. Break it down to one: SCP-1544 is 1 meter in height and has a mobile multi-colored skin covering controlled by a small peach-colored mass located in the center.

However, SCP-1544 will hibernate at temperatures exceeding 85° C, to preserve energy and prevent its interior from overheating.

Remove the second comma.

SCP-1544 will not return to it's usual behavior until music is played once more.

its, not it's. And "once more" isn't quite right tone-wise. Maybe "until music recommences"?

3. SCP-1544 has been proven to be able to consume plant life, though it will often throw the food through it's hands with what has been confirmed to be stomach bile.

its, not it's.

It just needs a bit of tightening up and fixing the grammar errors, but oh, poor Dr. Wondertainment. He just wants his buddy to be treated nice!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rephrased it for clarification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1544 has a jovial temperament, and appears to take great pleasure in dancing. SCP-1544 will playfully nudge and bump personnel, though it will not purposely cause harm. However, if music is not played in SCP-1544's containment chamber, it will display depression, sitting in the corner of its containment chamber.

This is actually bugging me the most. It never explicitly states in the description that the object only dances when music is being played. You can definitely infer that, but at the same time I think it should be stated.

Edit: And +1ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree. I think it gives us a very useful, albeit very limited, amount of insight into the Doctor himself. He obviously cared for this creature, so much so that he apparently sent it to the Circus for two reason: One, he knew it would be a place he could entertain people, make them happy. Two, he seems to have known the Foundation would find out eventualy, and that they would take care of him. It seems almost like Wondertainment looks at him like a son he isn't capable of taking care of, something that pains him. Which is why he doesn't want to hear about it anymore. It brings him that much grief. It humanizes Wondertainment, shows that he -really does- want to help people, even his creations, and that he holds *no* ill-will towards the Foundation, and views them as the second-best option for his inevitable failures, something I feel haunts him every day of his life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The premise is good, but it's not grabbing me as it should. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed object class from Euclid to Safe, as to better reflect the nature of the SCP itself.

http://i.imgur.com/JuHPQW2.gif

That gif is sorta a look at how the SCP would dance.
yeh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this gives away too much about how Wondertainment works without actually making said operations interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've seen other sapient organisms classified as Safe such as 343 and 999 and others. It really just seems like it should be Safe to me.
Classifications go off of what we know about it not what we don't know…
It just wants to dance!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Changed the containment procedures, to reflect why we play it music in the first place.

It's euclid, since it's a living organism, that may or may not be sapient. The foundation doesn't know what it will do (besides dance), so it's Euclid.

Aaaaand now we changed it to safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks man. I'm surprised we could even think up some kind of a hook that would carry it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am pleased my fuel made it all the way to an article of this caliber. Good work, both of you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Curious why it's necessary to play upbeat music for it in containment. Is there a side effect to it being down in the dumps?
I may also wonder why it is Euclid. Does it cause harm to people inadvertently? I always feel Dr Wondertainment products should almost always be Safe.
This is a cool SCP anyway.

Reminds me of http://www.staggeringbeauty.com/

Also there are some minor grammatical problems like in the containment procedures

nothing bad enough to deny my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Collaboration between Accelerando and I, after a night of drunken non content through IRC. Enjoy.
